paragraphs.text: Ironbark Supply Co. began in 2009 in a corrugated-iron shed on the outskirts of Ballarat, where founder Dale Ferrington spent his weekends re-handling old axes pulled from farm clearing-sales. What started as a one-man restoration habit grew, slowly and stubbornly, into a small workshop turning out tools for shearers, fencers, hikers and weekend bush-walkers across the Central Highlands.

Fifteen years on, we still operate from a workshop in Ballarat — larger now, with a forge, a leather bench, and a canvas room — but the brief has not changed. Build gear that earns its keep. Use materials you can name. Stand behind every piece that leaves the door.
We are a team of nine. We sharpen what we ship. We answer the phone when it rings.
We get asked a lot why our axes cost what they cost, and why our canvas bags weigh what they weigh. Here is the short version, in a table.
A small team does most of the work — forging, stitching, sharpening, packing. If you email us, one of these four is who you'll hear back from.
Workshop tours run Saturday mornings, 9am — by appointment. Tea provided.
Built for the bush since 2009. Ballarat, Victoria, Australia.

items.text:
Materials
          Hand-selected Australian hickory & Swedish high-carbon steel
          Mixed-source hardwoods, commodity carbon steel

Shipping
          100% carbon-offset, plastic-free packaging
          Standard freight, polywrap mailers

Guarantee
          Lifetime tool guarantee, free re-handling
          12–24 month limited warranty

Finishing
          Hand-ground bevels, linseed-oil finish
          Machine-ground, lacquer or paint

Manufacture
          Assembled in Ballarat, VIC
          Offshore contract manufacture

IRONBARK.
      Built for the bush since 2009. Ballarat, Victoria, Australia.

Shop
      
        All Products
        Cutting Tools
        Fire & Light
        Carry & Storage

Contact
      
        14 Forge Lane
        Ballarat VIC 3350
        hello@ironbark.au
        (03) 5331 0000

DF
        Dale Ferrington
        Founder & Head Smith
